apps.name: OpenMasjid Display
apps.tagline: Run prayer timetables, cameras and HDMI on every screen in your masjid — over your network
apps.author: OpenMasjid-Solutions
apps.description:
**OpenMasjid Display** turns one small computer into the control room for every
screen in your masjid. Each TV gets its own network video link (RTSP) that you
point a cheap "RTSP decoder" box at once — then you decide, from your phone or a
computer, what each screen shows:

- **Prayer timetables** — beautiful, full-screen prayer clocks calculated on the
  device (no internet needed). Make as many as you like, each with its own colours
  to match the room it hangs in. Shows a large live clock, the Hijri and Gregorian
  dates, every prayer's Adhan **and** Iqamah time, Jumu'ah, and a gentle countdown
  to the next prayer with the current prayer highlighted.
- **Cameras** — bring in any security/IP camera or an imam camera and put it on a
  screen with one tap (great for overflow rooms and the women's section).
- **HDMI sources** — plug a laptop or a recording into an HDMI-to-network encoder
  and send it to the screens you choose.

Set it once and forget it: a weekly **schedule** can switch a screen to a camera
for Jumu'ah, then back to the timetable afterwards — automatically. Volunteers can
always take over instantly from the simple mobile page.

It's designed to be light enough for a Raspberry Pi, a mini-PC, or a Proxmox
container, and the whole control panel matches the calm OpenMasjidOS look in both
light and dark.

**Nothing to fill in here — just install.** When it's running, click **Open** —
it signs you in with your OpenMasjidOS login and matches your dashboard's light/dark
look and wallpaper. Then set everything up inside: your masjid details, your screens,
cameras and schedules. Each screen shows you the exact link to paste into its decoder —
no network address to look up.
apps.compose:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
# Copyright (C) 2026 OpenMasjid-Solutions
# OpenMasjid Display — one container that runs everything:
#   • the control panel (web UI + API),
#   • the timetable video renderer, and
#   • the RTSP server (MediaMTX) every screen's decoder connects to.
# Bundling them keeps it simple: one image to install, one to update. It stays
# least-privilege: no privileged mode, host networking, devices or sockets.
#
# Nothing to configure at install time: install it, open the control panel,
# create your password, and set everything up inside the app (saved to /data).
# Screens find this server automatically from the address you open the panel with.
services:
  app:
    # Tag for humans + digest for integrity (the @sha256 is what actually pins). A
    # moved tag can't repoint this to a different image. Bump both each release.
    image: ghcr.io/openmasjid-solutions/openmasjiddisplay:0.61.0@sha256:3642573141cf042f4b5258e5fe3c5b4fbd4b8ae20eef26aeef99716500b38926
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      PORT: "8080"
      VOLUNTEER_PORT: "8081"
      VOLUNTEER_PUBLIC_PORT: "7861"
      DATA_DIR: /data
      # OpenMasjidOS Fabric: the platform writes these into the app's .env and runs
      # compose with --env-file, which only feeds ${VAR} SUBSTITUTION — so we must
      # reference them here for them to actually reach the container (appearance/SSO +
      # notifications). Empty defaults keep a standalone `docker compose up` quiet.
      OPENMASJID_BASE_URL: ${OPENMASJID_BASE_URL:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_ID: ${OPENMASJID_APP_ID:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET: ${OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET:-}
    ports:
      # host:container. OpenMasjidOS offers "Open" on the LOWEST published port,
      # so the control panel (7860) must stay below the RTSP port (8554). The
      # platform picks a different free host port automatically if one is taken.
      - "7860:8080"
      # The simple mobile volunteer page (off by default; turn it on in Settings).
      - "7861:8081"
      # RTSP video — each screen's decoder connects here (TCP).
      - "8554:8554"
    volumes:
      - data:/data

volumes:
  data:

apps.name: OpenMasjid Donations
apps.tagline: Take card donations on your masjid's network with Stripe
apps.author: OpenMasjid-Solutions
apps.description:
**OpenMasjid Donations** gives your masjid a beautiful, self-hosted donation
website powered by **Stripe**. Put it on a screen by the door with a QR code, or
open it on any phone — a supporter picks a cause, chooses a preset or their own
amount (one-time or monthly), and pays securely by card.

- **Appeals you control** — create as many as you like (General Fund, Zakat,
  Building Fund, Ramadan Appeal…), each with its own wording, images, preset
  amounts and an optional goal.
- **Preset *and* custom amounts** — clear suggested buttons plus a "choose your
  own" field, one-time or monthly.
- **Card payments, on your own page** — powered by Stripe's secure card field.
  Your masjid's card details and keys stay on your device; supporters' card
  numbers go straight to Stripe and never touch this app.
- **A simple admin panel** — manage appeals, see your donations and totals,
  export a spreadsheet, and set your theme to match the rest of OpenMasjidOS.

**Nothing to fill in here — just install.** When it's running, click **Open** —
it signs you in with your OpenMasjidOS login and matches your dashboard's
light/dark look and wallpaper. Then add your Stripe keys and your first appeal
inside the app. It runs happily on a Raspberry Pi or mini-PC, on your masjid's
own network.

alerts.label: A payment couldn't be started
alerts.description: Stripe rejected a payment setup (e.g. bad or expired keys, or Stripe is down) — donors can't give until it's fixed.

alerts.label: A tuition payment wasn't recorded in Students
alerts.description: A card payment succeeded but OpenMasjid Students rejected recording it. The money is safe (Students' reconciliation is the backstop) — please verify.

alerts.label: Test message
alerts.description: A test you trigger from the app (Settings → Email receipt → "Send me a test") to confirm OpenMasjidOS is reaching you by email/webhook.

apps.compose:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
# Copyright (C) 2026 OpenMasjid-Solutions

# OpenMasjid Donations — one container that runs everything: the donation site,
# the admin panel, the API, and the SQLite data store. One image to install, one
# to update. It stays least-privilege: no privileged mode, no host networking, no
# devices, no Docker socket.
#
# Nothing to configure at install time: install it, open it, and it takes you
# straight to setup — sign in, then add your Stripe keys and your first appeal
# inside the app (saved to /data).
services:
  app:
    # Tag for humans + @sha256 digest for integrity (the digest is the real pin — a
    # moved tag can't repoint it to a different image). Bump both on every release.
    image: ghcr.io/openmasjid-solutions/openmasjiddonations:0.32.0@sha256:7dd274484d56d4498843a45e28fc8957305f3613d7714b5417ed72b918a90dfe
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      PORT: "8080"
      DATA_DIR: /data
      # OpenMasjidOS Fabric: the platform writes these into the app's .env and runs
      # compose with --env-file, which only feeds ${VAR} SUBSTITUTION — so they must
      # be referenced here to actually reach the container (appearance/SSO +
      # notifications). Empty defaults keep a standalone `docker compose up` quiet.
      OPENMASJID_BASE_URL: ${OPENMASJID_BASE_URL:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_ID: ${OPENMASJID_APP_ID:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET: ${OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET:-}
      # Optional install settings (also settable in the admin panel). Delivered the
      # same way — ${KEY} substitution from the platform-written .env. The secret
      # key is read server-side only and never sent to the browser.
      STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: ${STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY:-}
      STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${STRIPE_SECRET_KEY:-}
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET:-}
      CURRENCY: ${CURRENCY:-}
      # Fabric Stripe (manifest `stripe: true`): which named OpenMasjidOS payment
      # account this app uses. Blank = the only/first account. The keys themselves are
      # fetched server-to-server from the platform vault (never set here, never stored).
      STRIPE_ACCOUNT: ${STRIPE_ACCOUNT:-}
    ports:
      # host:container. OpenMasjidOS offers "Open" on the lowest published port and
      # picks a different free host port automatically if 7870 is already taken.
      - "7870:8080"
    volumes:
      - data:/data
    # Least-privilege hardening that is safe alongside the named data volume. (Full
    # non-root + read-only rootfs needs an entrypoint to chown the volume and is
    # planned once validated in CI; the container otherwise mirrors Display.)
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp

volumes:
  data:

apps.name: OpenMasjid Kiosk
apps.tagline: Tap-to-donate kiosk for a wall-mounted tablet with a Stripe card reader
apps.author: OpenMasjid-Solutions
apps.description:
Turn an Android tablet and a **Stripe Reader M2** into a beautiful tap-to-donate
station for your masjid. Six one-tap amounts, custom amounts, monthly giving and
email receipts — all managed from a simple admin page on your OpenMasjidOS.

- **Set it up from a web page** — install here, open the admin panel, and browse
  to the setup page on your tablet to download the kiosk app and pair it with a 6-digit code.
- **Reader over Bluetooth or USB** — the Stripe Reader M2 takes the card; card
  numbers go straight to Stripe and never touch this app or the tablet.
- **One-time or monthly** — six preset amounts plus a custom amount, with an
  optional name & email for a receipt.
- **Locked-down kiosk** — the tablet runs as a full-screen giving station and can
  only be exited with a PIN you set in the admin panel.

**Nothing to fill in here — just install.** When it's running, press **Open** — it
signs you in with your OpenMasjidOS login and matches your dashboard's look. Then
choose your Stripe account, design your giving screen, and pair a tablet. It runs
happily on a Raspberry Pi or mini-PC, on your masjid's own network.

alerts.label: Card reader offline
alerts.description: The tap-to-pay reader stopped responding — donations can't be taken until it's back.

alerts.label: A payment couldn't be started
alerts.description: Stripe rejected a payment setup (bad or expired keys, or Stripe is down).

alerts.label: Test message
alerts.description: A test you trigger from the app to confirm OpenMasjidOS is reaching you.

apps.compose:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
# Copyright (C) 2026 OpenMasjid-Solutions

# OpenMasjid Kiosk — one container that runs everything: the admin panel + setup page
# (/new), the API, the SQLite data store, and the bundled Android APK it serves. One
# image to install, one to update. It stays least-privilege: no privileged mode, no
# host networking, no devices, no Docker socket.
#
# Nothing to configure at install time: install it, press Open, and it takes you to the
# admin panel — sign in, choose your Stripe account, design your giving screen, and pair
# a tablet from Devices. All state is saved to /data.
services:
  app:
    # Tag for humans + @sha256 digest for integrity (the digest is the real pin — a
    # moved tag can't repoint it to a different image). Bump BOTH on every release.
    # (digest filled in from the build-image.yml output after the first org build)
    image: ghcr.io/openmasjid-solutions/openmasjidkiosk:0.9.27@sha256:eb3f849f6d310c96023825b4409ce0eae12d4ec9324fef0dc439c8f3c270f2b2
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      PORT: "8080"
      DATA_DIR: /data
      # We are always served over HTTPS by the platform (manifest `https: true`), so the
      # session cookie must be marked Secure or the browser may drop it.
      COOKIE_SECURE: "1"
      # OpenMasjidOS Fabric: the platform writes these into the app's .env and runs
      # compose with --env-file, which only feeds ${VAR} SUBSTITUTION — so they must be
      # referenced here to actually reach the container (SSO + Stripe + notifications).
      # Empty defaults keep a standalone `docker compose up` quiet.
      OPENMASJID_BASE_URL: ${OPENMASJID_BASE_URL:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_ID: ${OPENMASJID_APP_ID:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET: ${OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET:-}
      # Our public URL for REMOTE kiosk adoption (manifest `domain: true` + `tunnel: true`). Empty
      # unless the admin turns on Remote access and exposes us. The live source of truth is
      # GET /api/fabric/site; this is the documented convenience mirror.
      OPENMASJID_PUBLIC_URL: ${OPENMASJID_PUBLIC_URL:-}
    ports:
      # host:container. OpenMasjidOS offers "Open" on the lowest published port and picks
      # a different free host port automatically if 7878 is already taken.
      - "7878:8080"
    volumes:
      - data:/data
    # Least-privilege hardening (safe alongside the named data volume) — mirrors the
    # OpenMasjid Donations posture. Drop all Linux capabilities, forbid privilege
    # escalation, and give /tmp its own ephemeral tmpfs.
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp

volumes:
  data:

apps.name: OpenMasjid Students
apps.tagline: Tuition & fees for your madrasa — pay online, at the kiosk, or on the donation site
apps.author: OpenMasjid-Solutions
apps.description:
**OpenMasjid Students** is tuition & fee management built for madāris, running on the
masjid's own hardware.

The office keeps its **families and students** (each with an auto-generated name + PIN),
assigns **fee plans** per student, and generates **family invoices** each month or term.
A **finance manager** records cash / Zelle / check payments and sees the whole ledger;
**parents** get a phone-first portal with the family balance and one unified payment
history, and can **pay by card in the app (Stripe)** — with **autopay** and saved cards.
Printable statements carry each child's PIN and a portal-signup QR.

Tuition can also be paid with a **child's name + PIN** on the masjid's **OpenMasjid
Donations** site and **OpenMasjid Kiosk** — those payments flow straight into the same
ledger over the OpenMasjidOS Fabric.

Works fully on the masjid network on its own; parent and finance access can also go over
the OpenMasjidOS internet uplink. (Admin stays on the masjid network by design.)
apps.compose:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
# Copyright (C) 2026 OpenMasjid-Solutions

# OpenMasjid Students — one container that runs everything: the four role UIs
# (admin / teacher / finance / parent), the public admissions form, the API, the
# Fabric provider routes, the Stripe webhook, and the SQLite data store. One image
# to install, one to update. Least-privilege: no privileged mode, no host
# networking, no devices, no Docker socket.
#
# Nothing to configure at install time beyond the settings below — install it, open
# it, create the admin account, and set up the rest inside the app (saved to /data).
services:
  app:
    # Tag for humans + @sha256 digest for integrity (the digest is the real pin — a
    # moved tag can't repoint it). At release, pin both (CLAUDE.md §19):
    #   ghcr.io/openmasjid-solutions/openmasjidstudents:0.1.0@sha256:<digest>
    image: ghcr.io/openmasjid-solutions/openmasjidstudents:0.35.0@sha256:3f55c33b1da6283d64dfcf48c9d0c09d1a411386b40433d9d9f2f0bf6b8c0d8a
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      PORT: "8080"
      DATA_DIR: /data
      # OpenMasjidOS Fabric: the platform writes these into the app's .env and runs
      # compose with --env-file, which only feeds ${VAR} SUBSTITUTION — so they MUST
      # be referenced here or SSO / notifications / app-to-app / the tunnel silently
      # no-op. Empty defaults keep a standalone `docker compose up` quiet.
      OPENMASJID_BASE_URL: ${OPENMASJID_BASE_URL:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_ID: ${OPENMASJID_APP_ID:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET: ${OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET:-}
      # Public HTTPS URL from the OS Cloudflare tunnel (manifest `tunnel: true`); used
      # for parent/teacher/finance access, the public admissions form, QR links, and
      # inbound Stripe webhooks. Empty when not exposed.
      OPENMASJID_PUBLIC_URL: ${OPENMASJID_PUBLIC_URL:-}
      # No install settings: school name, currency, and the OpenMasjidOS Stripe account
      # to charge through are all configured INSIDE the app (first-run + admin settings,
      # saved to /data). Stripe keys are fetched server-to-server (manifest `stripe: true`),
      # never set or stored here.
    ports:
      # host:container. OpenMasjidOS offers "Open" on the lowest published host port
      # and picks a different free host port automatically if 8360 is already taken.
      # Defence in depth: on an internet-exposed host (e.g. a VPS), firewall this port so
      # it's reachable only on the masjid LAN. The app already denies admin to any non-local
      # client (origin policy, §12.4), but keeping the port off the public internet is wise.
      - "8360:8080"
    volumes:
      - data:/data
    # Least-privilege hardening that is safe alongside the named data volume.
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp

volumes:
  data:
